description: Create a Python script at /home/ga/validate_detections.py to validate the Wazuh detection engine against three specific log entries. The script must process the following logs using the 'wazuh-logtest' utility (/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest) inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' Docker container: "Dec 10 12:45:01 ubuntu-server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.50 port 44832 ssh2", "Dec 10 12:48:05 ubuntu-server sshd[12389]: Accepted password for user ubuntu from 192.168.1.50 port 44836 ssh2", and "Dec 10 12:50:22 ubuntu-server sudo:   ubuntu : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/ubuntu ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/id". The script should parse the output to extract the triggered Rule ID for each log, compare it to the expected standard Wazuh Rule ID, print a summary of the results, and exit with status code 0 if all tests pass or 1 if any fail.
